channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Direita racha, PL pressiona PSD e abre caminho para Gleisi no Paraná
item.description: The surprise affiliation of journalist Cristina Graeml with the PSD in Paraná has shaken the state's political landscape, following her departure from União Brasil, which had offered her a federal deputy seat — as had the PL. Her move opens the possibility for her to run for Senate or serve as vice on Governor Ratinho Jr.'s ticket, who leads the PSD in the state. This realignment weakens right-wing coordination in Paraná and could create opportunities for candidates like Gleisi Hoffmann of the PT by deepening divisions among opposition forces.

item.title: Prefeita petista desbanca ministro de Lula em disputa por Senado em MG
item.description: Former petista mayor of Contagem, Marília Campos, is leading Senate polls in Minas Gerais, challenging Minister Alexandre Silveira (PSD), who lost PT support and decided to run for reasons beyond loyalty to Lula. Her rise reflects the PSD's distancing from the federal government and the strengthening of the PT candidacy in the state.

item.title: É #FAKE que Lula deu bolsa de R$ 7 mil por mês e moradia gratuita a refugiados muçulmanos no Brasil
item.description: The claim that the Lula government granted monthly stipends of R$ 7,000 and free housing to Muslim refugees is false. The video shared in the post actually shows the repatriation of Brazilians from Lebanon in October 2024, linked to Operation Acolhida, which assists Venezuelan migrants. The Ministry of Social Development confirmed there is no federal program offering benefits based on religion or nationality.

item.title: Aprecie com moderação
item.description: Despite recent improvements in educational indicators such as Saresp and literacy rates in the second grade, results remain insufficient and below expectations, reflecting a pattern of modest progress in a system where assessments are calibrated too low. Electoral governments tend to exaggerate these gains with celebratory rhetoric, but experts warn this normalizes inadequate standards and overlooks that many students still fall short of basic proficiency, even by international benchmarks.

item.title: Tarcísio aposta em entregas do metrô para viralizar no ano eleitoral
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) is betting on the delivery of transportation infrastructure, particularly the Line 17-Gold of the São Paulo Metro, to strengthen his image in the 2026 electoral campaign, using humorous and viral videos on social media, including references to the 2014 World Cup mascot Fuleco and anime aesthetics. The strategy aims to counter the Workers' Party narrative, which emphasizes federal funding for the projects, while Tarcísio's allies believe his positive delivery agenda could secure a first-round victory against Fernando Haddad (PT), given the absence of other strong candidates.

item.title: PEC da Segurança segue parada no Senado um mês após passar na Câmara
item.description: The Public Security PEC (18/2025), approved in the Chamber of Deputies one month ago, remains stalled in the Senate due to the lack of a rapporteur designation by Senate President Davi Alcolumbre. The delay stems from growing tension between Alcolumbre and President Lula following the appointment of Jorge Messias to the STF, which undermined Alcolumbre’s preferred candidate. Unlike the Chamber, the Senate has no fixed procedure, giving Alcolumbre discretion to accelerate or delay the bill, which is a federal government priority in this election year.

item.title: As regras do jogo: o que muda para o Tribunal Superior Eleitoral em 2026 - O Assunto #1694
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) has released the definitive rules for the 2026 elections, banning payment for political electoral content on social media and reversing the burden of proof in cases of improper AI use, requiring producers of false content to technically prove no fraud occurred. The Court will be presided over by Minister Kassio Nunes Marques, with André Mendonça as vice-president, both appointed to the STF by Jair Bolsonaro and known for their discreet profiles. Additional rules restrict AI use three days before voting and prohibit platforms from suggesting candidates.

item.title: Filme traz de volta suspeitas sobre mortes na ditadura
item.description: The article revisits the Bolsonarist narrative linking João Goulart to communism due to his 1961 visit to China, coinciding with Jânio Quadros' resignation. It contrasts this with Richard Nixon, a U.S. leader involved in corruption scandals, who also pursued relations with China in 1972. The text suggests the 1964 military coup that toppled Goulart was backed and financed by the United States, offering a revisionist view of Brazil's dictatorship.

item.title: Quem são os governadores que deixaram mandatos para disputar a eleição
item.description: Eleven governors resigned from their offices to run in the 2026 elections, including two presidential candidates (Ronaldo Caiado and Romeu Zema) and eight seeking Senate seats. The resignation is required by law to prevent misuse of public resources in campaigns, with vice governors assuming the roles. Official candidacies will be confirmed in August after party conventions and TSE registration.

item.title: Sob pressão, governo promete editar MP de nova subvenção do diesel nos próximos dias
item.description: The Lula government promises to issue a provisional measure on diesel subsidies in the coming days, in partnership with states, to reduce the gap between international prices and domestic caps, with a fiscal impact estimated between R$ 3.5 and R$ 4 billion. The measure aims to overcome resistance from major distributors like Vibra (BR, Ipiranga, Raízen) and Petrobras, which initially rejected the first subsidy program, while only Rio de Janeiro and Rondônia have refused to join so far, with two to three other states still evaluating.

item.title: Estímulos à economia devem ultrapassar R$ 700 bi em 2026, ano eleitoral
item.description: In 2026, an election year, economic stimulus in Brazil is projected to reach R$ 742 billion, a 139% increase from 2025, equivalent to 5.4% of GDP.

item.title: CNDH pede investigação de juiz e advogado por homenagem ao golpe de 64
item.description: The CNDH has requested the OAB and TJ-PB to investigate a lawyer and a judge for honoring the 1964 military coup during an online session of the Criminal Chamber of the Paraíba State Court. The body considers the apology for the dictatorship a violation of human rights and the Brazilian Constitution.

item.title: Red Bull Bragantino vence de novo, e Mirassol completa 11 jogos sem vitória
item.description: Red Bull Bragantino defeated Mirassol 1-0 at Maião, ending Mirassol's 11-match winless streak in the 2026 Brasileirão. Mirassol sits at the bottom with six points and faces relegation threats, while Bragantino rises to 14 points in ninth place. The only goal came in the 50th minute after a corner kick and header by Isidro Pitta.

item.title: Dorival Júnior não resiste à nova derrota e é demitido do Corinthians
item.description: Corinthians fired head coach Dorival Júnior following a 1-0 loss to Internacional at Neo Química Arena, extending the team's winless streak to nine games. Dorival managed 63 matches with 25 wins, 19 draws, and 19 losses, leaving behind achievements including the 2025 Copa do Brasil and 2026 Supercopa Rei. Training on Monday will be led by William Batista, the under-20 team coach.

item.title: MPE recorre de decisão de Zanin que anulou condenação de Garotinho
item.description: The Electoral Public Prosecutor's Office appealed the interim decision by Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin that annulled the conviction of former governor Anthony Garotinho in the 'Chequinho' operation. Garotinho had been sentenced to 13 years and 9 months in prison for an alleged scheme to buy political support. The MPE's appeal seeks to overturn the suspension of the conviction's effects.

item.title: Lula 3 falha em popularidade porque perdeu a chance de surpreender
item.description: Lula’s third term maintains political stability and decent economic indicators, yet fails to generate electoral enthusiasm by not surprising Brazilians. His popularity is declining because he is perceived as maintaining the status quo rather than driving transformation, despite fulfilling basic promises and avoiding institutional crises.

item.title: Governo busca substituto para Gleisi; STF define regras para eleições no RJ
item.description: The government is seeking a replacement for Minister Gleisi Hoffmann following the departure of ministers who will run in the October elections, including Geraldo Alckmin. The STF has established the rules for the elections in Rio de Janeiro, setting guidelines for the state-level vote. The post-holiday week in Brasília will be marked by executive reorganization amid ongoing candidacies.

item.title: Trump estende prazo para o Irã reabrir estreito de Ormuz
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump extended the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz until 9:00 p.m. Brasília time on April 7, 2026, after initially setting a 48-hour deadline from April 4. The strait, through which about 20% of global oil flows, has been closed amid U.S.-Israeli military actions against Iran since February 28, 2026, causing global energy market volatility and rising fuel prices. Iran has refused to comply unilaterally, demanding political and security guarantees before any retreat, while Trump faces domestic pressure ahead of the November 2026 midterm elections.

item.title: Garotinho protagoniza propaganda partidária do Republicanos
item.description: Anthony Garotinho, former governor of Rio de Janeiro and 2026 pre-candidate for the Republicans party, starred in a party advertisement claiming it's time to put 'courageous people' to face a 'criminal gang' and return the state to the people. Despite being sentenced to 13 years for vote-buying in the Chequinho operation, his ineligibility was annulled by Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin, allowing him to run. A Gerp poll shows him with 8% voting intentions, tied for second place.

item.title: Renato Gaúcho vira assunto após derrota do Vasco para o Botafogo: ‘Incoerente’
item.description: Renato Gaúcho suffered his first loss as Vasco’s head coach after the team was overturned by Botafogo in the 10th round of the Brasileirão. The defeat sparked criticism on social media, with fans accusing him of incoherence and poor tactical preparation, particularly for not using all substitutions and lacking sufficient game analysis. Despite initial support, his tenure now faces scrutiny over whether he can sustain early success.

item.title: Janja cozinha paca para almoço de Páscoa com Lula; assista
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva prepared paca meat for President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's Easter lunch, sharing a video on Instagram. Lula praised the dish as 'divine' and said he had never tasted paca so delicious. Janja concluded the video by requesting to appear on Ana Maria Braga's morning show on TV Globo.

item.title: Janja, Lula e a carne de paca
item.description: First Lady Janja da Silva posted an Easter Sunday video showing her preparing paca meat for President Lula, stating it had been marinated for two days with green seasoning, garlic, and herbs. Lula appears in the video commenting on the dish, highlighting the familial and cultural context of the meal.

item.title: Brasiliense empata com o Primavera-MT na estreia da Série D 2026. Veja vídeo
item.description: Brasiliense drew 1-1 with Primavera-MT at home in their 2026 Série D opener. Regino scored for Brasiliense, while Giovanni equalized for Primavera-MT in the 26th minute. Brasiliense dominated possession and created multiple chances but failed to secure a win.

item.title: Após bate-boca entre Eduardo Bolsonaro e Nikolas Ferreira, Michelle compartilha vídeo de deputado
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro shared a video by Nikolas Ferreira about the film 'The Passion of the Christ', following a social media clash between Eduardo Bolsonaro and Ferreira. Eduardo criticized Ferreira for disrespecting his family and allegedly working against former president Jair Bolsonaro, claiming the deputy has strayed from their shared values. The dispute is unrelated to the content Michelle reposted.

item.title: Michelle mostra almoço de Páscoa na casa dos Bolsonaros; assista
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro shared an Instagram video showing Easter lunch at the Bolsonaro residence in Brasília, highlighting a Le Creuset pot used to cook rice and cod. Former president Jair Bolsonaro has been under house arrest since March 27, following a 90-day grant by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes. The Le Creuset pot featured in the video ranges from R$1,579 to R$4,399 on the brand’s website.

item.title: Vorcaro relatou ter recebido ACM Neto em casa
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, from Banco Master, stated he received former Salvador mayor ACM Neto (União Brasil) at his home in May 2024, according to messages sent to ex-minister Fábio Faria. Screenshots of these conversations, stored in Apple’s cloud, were disclosed by Folha de S.Paulo. The meeting took place amid political maneuvering involving centrist and opposition figures.

item.title: Best-seller da autoajuda, Augusto Cury se filia a partido e confirma candidatura à Presidência
item.description: Best-selling self-help author Augusto Cury, known for 'The Dreams Seller' and with over 45 million books sold, has joined the Avante party and confirmed his pre-candidacy for the 2026 Brazilian presidential election. His platform focuses on ending political polarization and boosting industrial productivity through artificial intelligence, following meetings with figures such as Michel Temer, Gilberto Kassab, Aécio Neves, and Renata Abreu. However, recent polls show the presidential race is largely consolidated between Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL), with other candidates collectively polling below 10% in voter intentions.

item.title: Em três meses, governo Lula pune mais de 200 servidores por ilegalidades na máquina federal
item.description: In three months, the Lula government punished 203 federal officials for irregularities, with 79 dismissed and 97 suspended, according to CGU data. The actions aim to combat misconduct across various public agencies. The CGU is the body responsible for investigating and sanctioning public servants and companies involved in illegal acts within the civil service.

item.title: Augusto Cury anuncia pré-candidatura à Presidência
item.description: Writer and psychiatrist Augusto Cury announced his affiliation with the Avante party and his launch as a pre-candidate for President of Brazil in the 2026 elections. In his statement, he said his goal is to contribute to building the Brazil of our dreams, emphasizing that he does not seek power for power's sake. The announcement was made on Sunday, October 5, and was first reported by O Antagonista.

item.title: Lula segura Silveira no governo por Messias no STF e Pacheco em MG
item.description: Lula asked Minister Alexandre Silveira to remain in government to prevent him from running for the Senate in Minas Gerais, which could strain relations with Davi Alcolumbre and jeopardize Jorge Messias' STF nomination. The move also accommodates Rodrigo Pacheco, who opposes sharing a ticket with Silveira. Silveira will now serve as a key liaison with Congress following the loss of other top Planalto channels.

item.title: Após Eduardo Bolsonaro atacar Nikolas, Michelle posta vídeo do deputado
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro shared a video of deputy Nikolas Ferreira in support of him after criticism from her brother-in-law, Eduardo Bolsonaro. The conflict arose after Eduardo accused Nikolas of insufficiently supporting the presidential campaign of his brother, Flávio Bolsonaro, and of giving space on social media to profiles that do not promote the candidacy. The exchange of barbs among Jair Bolsonaro’s family members highlights internal divisions within the Brazilian right, with Flávio attempting to mediate the dispute.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro pede união na direita depois de farpas entre Eduardo e Nikolas
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, senator and pre-candidate for president in 2026, called for unity on the Brazilian right following an exchange of barbs between Eduardo Bolsonaro and Nikolas Ferreira. He urged right-wing politicians to stop 'fighting' over 'grudges and provocations' and focus on the country's future.

item.title: A nova pesquisa Datafolha para à Presidência, a primeira depois do lançamento de Caiado
item.description: Datafolha will conduct an electoral poll between Tuesday and Thursday to measure voting intentions for the Presidency of the Republic, with results to be released on Saturday, the 11th. This is the institute’s first poll since Ronaldo Caiado was officially named the PSD candidate and after the end of the incompatibilization period. The article is subscriber-only and requires registration for full access.

item.title: Relator do caso Master, Mendonça é homenageado em evento com Tarcísio
item.description: André Mendonça, the Supreme Court justice overseeing the Master and INSS cases, will be honored with the Legislative Merit Medal at the São Paulo State Assembly, attended by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas and other officials. The recognition comes as bolsonarists leverage his rulings—such as the arrest of banker Daniel Vorcaro and the lifting of financial secrecy on Lula’s son—to attack Alexandre de Moraes and President Lula.

item.title: Avante anuncia pré-candidatura do escritor Augusto Cury à Presidência da República
item.description: The Avante party announced the pre-candidacy of writer Augusto Cury for President of Brazil in the 2026 elections, emphasizing emotional balance, education, and humanized governance. Cury, a physician and psychologist, is a best-selling author who states his candidacy is a collective journey, not a pursuit of power. He joins other pre-candidates including Lula, Caiado, Flávio Bolsonaro, Zema, Renan Santos, and Aldo Rebelo.

item.title: A turbulenta relação entre dois ministros importantes de Lula no governo
item.description: The relationship between Defense Minister José Múcio and Chief of Staff Rui Costa deteriorated after Costa overlooked the Defense Ministry's achievements in a report presented to Lula. Múcio reacted sharply, claiming his ministry had more accomplishments than the Ministry of Women, during a ministerial meeting. Despite the tension, Múcio remains in government due to his personal commitment to Lula, as the country faces growing global armed conflicts requiring a strong military agenda.

item.title: Janja celebra a Páscoa com cachorras fantasiadas de coelhinhas
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva shared an AI-generated photo of her three dogs dressed as bunny girls to celebrate Easter, wishing love, smiles, and unity. She also appeared in a post by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who emphasized renewal and family peace. The post was made on Sunday, April 5, 2026, on Instagram.

item.title: Sem propostas por ora, Flávio Bolsonaro e Ronaldo Caiado se apoiam em anistia aos golpistas
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro and Ronaldo Caiado, 2026 presidential pre-candidates, converge on proposing amnesty for the January 8 rioters, including Jair Bolsonaro, as a strategy to capture Bolsonarist voters. Both adopt a frontal opposition stance to Lula’s government, with Caiado emphasizing his executive experience and Flávio seeking to preserve Bolsonaro’s legacy, while competing for influence in agribusiness and evangelical electorates.

item.title: Ala do PT contesta filiação de Kátia Abreu
item.description: A minority faction of the PT is challenging the affiliation of Kátia Abreu, arguing that the process violated internal procedures by bypassing state directory deliberation and criticizing her agribusiness background as incompatible with the party's principles. Despite the challenge, the national leadership is expected to uphold her membership, as Abreu states she intends to support Lulu in defending democracy and his reelection bid.

item.title: MPE recorre de decisão do STF que liberou candidatura de Garotinho no RJ
item.description: The Electoral Public Ministry (MPE) appealed the Supreme Federal Court's (STF) decision that suspended the electoral effects of former Rio de Janeiro governor Anthony Garotinho's conviction, thereby allowing his candidacy. The MPE contends that the STF ruling violates the Clean Record Law and seeks to overturn the benefit granted to the politician.

item.title: Páscoa é momento de renovação e união, diz Lula em mensagem
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva released an Easter message emphasizing renewal and family unity, posted alongside First Lady Janja and children. Vice President Geraldo Alckmin also commented, framing Easter as a celebration of God's love and an opportunity to strengthen familial bonds. Both messages were shared on social media this Sunday (April 5).

item.title: STF condena a 14 anos de prisão empresário catarinense que doou R$ 500 para ônibus fretado para 8 de janeiro
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) sentenced Santa Catarina businessman Alcides Hahn to 14 years in prison for transferring R$500 to fund a chartered bus that transported demonstrators from Blumenau to Brasília on January 8, 2023. Hahn was convicted of violent abolition of the democratic rule of law, coup d’état, qualified damage, deterioration of public heritage, and criminal association. His defense filed an appeal, which was removed from the docket on March 20.

item.title: A forma que Geraldo Alckmin encontrou de tentar ajudar Fernando Haddad na eleição
item.description: Geraldo Alckmin, now Lula's running mate, is leveraging his position to boost Fernando Haddad's campaign in São Paulo. He has been calling mayors in the state's interior, promising the release of parliamentary amendments in exchange for support for the petista candidate. Party leaders view the effort skeptically, noting that while the funds are available, the political credit will go to Lula, not Haddad.

item.title: Eduardo Bolsonaro ataca Nikolas Ferreira e diz que 'fama fez mal' ao deputado
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro publicly criticized Nikolas Ferreira on X, stating that 'fame did him harm'. The exchange reflects growing tensions between different factions of Brazil's far-right. The episode highlights internal divisions within Bolsonarism ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Wilson Lima renuncia como governador do Amazonas e cita prazo de desincompatibilização
item.description: The governor of Amazonas, Wilson Lima (União Brasil), resigned from office on the night of April 4 through a letter published in the Official Gazette of the State Legislative Assembly (ALEAM). He stated the decision is irrevocable and aimed at fulfilling the electoral law's disassociation requirement, likely in preparation for a potential candidacy in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Políticos publicam mensagens de Páscoa nas redes sociais
item.description: Brazilian politicians, including President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, First Lady Janja Lula da Silva, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, former governor Romeu Zema, and others, posted Easter messages on social media on Sunday, April 5, 2026. The posts emphasized themes of renewal, family unity, Christian faith, solidarity, and hope, reflecting the ideological leanings of each figure.

item.title: A 6 meses das eleições, Janja prioriza pautas relacionadas a mulheres
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva has prioritized women's issues in her agenda, with 31 of 124 disclosed commitments between October 2025 and April 2026 related to gender equality, reflecting the government's electoral strategy. Her work includes leading the National Pact to Combat Femicide and influencing Lula’s stronger stance on gender-based violence following public emotional displays. She also focuses on environmental protection (22 commitments) and international engagements (19), including a trip to Asia for the Brazil-Korea Business Forum.

item.title: Janja soma 170 dias fora do Brasil desde 2023
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva has spent 170 days abroad since 2023, including 13 days in 2026 across three international trips to South Korea, the UAE, and the United States. Her March 8–13 visit to New York included participation in the UN’s 70th Commission on the Status of Women, focusing on gender and social policies, reinforcing her role as a key advocate in these areas. With women comprising 53% of the Brazilian electorate ahead of the 2026 elections, her agenda has prioritized initiatives like the National Pact to Combat Femicide.

item.title: Michelle é uma unha encravada nos pés de barro dos enteados
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro is portrayed as a strict stepmother who delayed her involvement in Flávio Bolsonaro’s pre-campaign and is now dismantling narratives crafted by Carlos and Eduardo Bolsonaro that frame jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro as a political victim.

item.title: Botafogo provoca Vasco após vitória no clássico pelo Brasileirão; veja
item.description: Botafogo defeated Vasco 2-1 at São Januário in the 10th round of the 2026 Brasileirão, then provoked the rival on social media with references to Vasco's 15-year title drought, the 'bacalhau' nickname, and Easter. Posts included messages like 'Calma, Família! Essa dança aqui precisa de cadência' and 'FELIZ PÁSCOA, FAMÍLIA BOTAFOGO!', along with nods to Vasco's historical edge in the fixture. The taunts followed Botafogo's comeback win, with the club declaring 'virou lição' (lesson learned).
